title: Optional rest and wake contract
description: Define when rest is allowed, refused, interrupted, or advances ordinary turns.

title: Timer advancement and exploit prevention
description: Preserve canonical queues and prevent skipping danger or farming events.

title: Bounded nonterminal recovery
description: Recover selected temporary states without erasing permanent consequences.

title: Discovery-driven dream records
description: Generate authored dreams from objects, actors, rituals, follies, and unresolved evidence.

title: Overnight house changes
description: Apply mail, visits, theft, damp, smoke, movement, and archive updates deterministically.

title: Noises, warnings, and forced waking
description: Wake the player for authored danger or evidence while preserving escape routes.

title: Rest, dream, and persistence qualification
description: Prove timers, recovery, dreams, interruptions, ordering, and restore.

title: Bedroom rest and dreams capstone
description: Document optionality and connect overnight evidence to the archive.

title: House damage and condition transitions
description: Model a small authored set of clean, disturbed, smoky, damp, damaged, and supernatural states.

title: Smoke, damp, and cellar-water propagation
description: Connect fire, chimney, leak, reservoir, and carried-water states to bounded consequences.

title: Thief burglary and display disturbance
description: Use canonical thief capability against unsecured collections with recoverable evidence.

title: Creature and follower intrusion
description: Allow selected actors to follow scent, sound, objects, or open routes home.

title: Mirror, shadow, ritual, and cursed-object effects
description: Let dangerous artifacts alter light, reflections, sounds, or dreams.

title: Meaningful repair without chores
description: Resolve damage through specific objects, knowledge, or consequences, not meters.

title: Vulnerability gameplay and persistence
description: Prove trigger order, containment, repair, identity, archive evidence, and restore.

title: House vulnerability capstone
description: Integrate condition with museum, cellar, rest, and archive records.

title: Victory-gated expedition record
description: Unlock the master archive after genuine completion while preserving partial pre-victory files.

title: Chronological route and incident timeline
description: Reconstruct rooms, discoveries, puzzles, deaths, follies, encounters, and returns home.

title: Final world and house state summary
description: Record mechanisms, actors, treasure, altered objects, letters, archive, and home state.

title: Separate expedition boxes
description: Store completed playthroughs separately so mutually exclusive histories never merge.

title: Cross-run comparison
description: Compare actor outcomes, area resolutions, object fates, route order, mail, and house.

title: Unseen and alternate-outcome boundaries
description: Indicate alternatives exist without revealing commands, solutions, or unseen text.

title: Archive versioning, compatibility, and export
description: Migrate schemas and produce deterministic human-readable expedition receipts.

title: House of Records program capstone
description: Run whole-game and multi-run qualification, reconcile all trains, and publish final docs.
